coreTruthSet.description: Conflict is framed as an inevitable systemic condition in dense multi-party networks, not as an abnormal exception.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Conflict is not abnormal. It is inevitable.

coreTruthSet.description: The stated governance goal is conflict management that preserves system integrity rather than conflict elimination.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The goal is not to eliminate conflict... manage it without breaking the system.

coreTruthSet.description: Miscommunication (language, cultural assumptions, tone) is described as the most common proximate spark for escalation.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Most conflict doesn’t begin with intent. It begins with misunderstanding.

coreTruthSet.description: Resource shocks (harvest failure, flooding, drought, overhunting) are treated as primary escalation triggers from tension to survival conflict.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: People don’t fight because they want to... because they have to survive.

coreTruthSet.description: Trade stability depends on perceived fairness and reciprocity; exploitation perception leads to refusal, distrust, and retaliation.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Trade must feel fair—even if it isn’t equal.

settingAnchors.name: Conflict Pressure Web (Red River Network)
settingAnchors.description: System-level theater where resource, trade, language, and kinship tensions accumulate and are either managed or destabilized.

continuityConstraints.title: Meaning Correction Before Emotion
continuityConstraints.text: De-escalation depends on correcting meaning early, before emotional momentum hardens misunderstanding into retaliation.

continuityConstraints.title: Preemptive Relief Before Violence
continuityConstraints.text: Legitimate power acts before scarcity-driven desperation converts into open violence; timing is the core trust mechanic.

continuityConstraints.title: Kinship Obligation Pressure
continuityConstraints.text: Kinship strengthens cooperation while simultaneously increasing obligation strain; unmet expectations can destabilize alliances.

openResearchGaps.title: How were competing kinship obligations adjudicated when resource or honor claims collided?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to model internal fractures without flattening kin networks into simple loyalty blocs.

openResearchGaps.title: What concrete thresholds separate manageable scarcity from violence-prone desperation in this network?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to calibrate when mediation can still work versus when emergency intervention is required.

openResearchGaps.title: Which practical mechanisms made trade feel fair across culturally asymmetric parties?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for credible conflict-resolution scenes around reciprocity and value translation.
